fact: May 16 is exactly 136 days into the year - that's almost exactly one-third of the way through!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby birds to hatch - you might hear tons of chirping from nests right now!
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy around this date, turning from yellow flowers into those white puff balls you can blow everywhere.
The sun rises super early now - in some northern places, it's already light by 5:30 AM!
May 16 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season, which means the bull constellation is ruling the zodiac right now.
Earthworms are having a party in the soil - they're most active during these warm, moist spring days.
Tree leaves have that bright, almost glowing green color that only happens in mid-May before summer makes them darker.
The days are getting longer by about 2 minutes every single day around this time in most places!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere spots, May 16 is deep in autumn - leaves are turning orange and red while we're getting green!
Bees are working overtime right now because so many flowers are blooming at once - it's like an all-you-can-eat buffet for them.
The moon phase around May 16 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Flower Moon because of all the blooms.
Grass grows so fast in mid-May that some people have to mow their lawns twice a week to keep up!
